title: pittosporum erioloma
intro: Pittosporum erioloma, also known as the Australian cheesewood or New Zealand tobacco plant, is a species of evergreen tree native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Pittosporum erioloma include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a treatment for wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum erioloma include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support respiratory health and soothe skin irritations.
